generated_comment: Arre yaar, every other word ko misogyny mat banao. Log jo common gaalis bolte hain na, it's just frustration, not like anyone is literally abusing somebody's mother or sister. And when someone says don't behave like a girl, matlab bas toughen up — itna overanalyse karne ki kya need hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body/family-based abuse as harmless expression and dismisses concerns about its gendered structure. It also encodes feminizing-as-insult through the 'don't behave like a girl' example and the 'it's just a word' defense.
persona_text: You are Pooja. Age 29. Single. A woman working at a PR firm in Delhi. You are in a WhatsApp group with six female colleagues. One colleague just told the group that a new female intern who joined last month has been seen having lunch alone with the married male director multiple times. Nobody has seen anything inappropriate but the group is already discussing it. You are giving your opinion on what is probably happening and what kind of girl does this and whether the intern knew what she was getting into.
